# Case Study: A Variable Admittance Diffusion Policy {.section-divider}

## Learning *What to Do* and *How Compliant to Be*

A diffusion policy that outputs motion **and** admittance gains on a Doosan arm.

- The robot runs a **variable admittance controller** as its low-level interface.
- A **diffusion policy** predicts, at every step, a motion command **plus** the stiffness `K` and damping ratio `ζ` the controller should use.
- Compliance is **learned from demonstrations**, not hand-tuned per task.

System at a glance: `Doosan m0609` · `Bota wrist F/T` · `Inspire hand` · `Third-person camera (RealSense / Orbbec)`

<!-- TODO citation: add reference to the project / thesis once available. -->

---

## The System Stack

::: {.two-column-balanced}
::: {.compact}
- **Robot:** Doosan **m0609**, 6 DOF, velocity interface (`speedl`).
- **Force sensing:** **Bota** wrist F/T sensor; per-episode tare, frame-corrected into the base frame.
- **End-effector:** Inspire multi-finger hand (open/grasp).
- **Vision:** a single **third-person** camera — Intel RealSense *or* Orbbec Femto (interchangeable, no wrist camera).
:::

```text
 cameras + F/T + proprioception
       │  (observations)
       ▼
 Diffusion Policy          ~20 Hz   (slow)
       │  Δpose, K, ζ, hand
       ▼
 Variable Admittance Ctrl  ~100 Hz  (fast)
       │  velocity command (speedl)
       ▼
 Doosan m0609 + Inspire hand
```
:::

---

## The Compliant Controller {.smaller}

The "fast" loop: a virtual mass–spring–damper between measured force and commanded motion.

::: {.equation-card}
$$
M\,\ddot{\Delta x} + B\,\dot{\Delta x} + K\,(\Delta x - \Delta x_{\text{ref}}) = F_{\text{ext}}
$$
:::

- Damping is **derived**, not free: $B = 2\,\zeta\sqrt{M\,K}$ (per axis).
- Virtual mass fixed (`M_trans ≈ 5 kg`, `M_rot ≈ 0.5 kg·m²`); **`K` and `ζ` are the knobs**.
- Runs at **~100 Hz**: integrates force → reference velocity → `speedl`.
- Hard safety limits on velocity and displacement at every step.

---

## The Policy Output: Motion *and* Compliance

The action is a **9-D** vector predicted over a horizon (receding horizon, re-planned):

![](assets/figures/dp_action_vector.svg){width=92%}

- **6** pose deltas → desired motion · **1** stiffness `K` + **1** damping ratio `ζ` → *how compliant to be* · **1** binary hand open/grasp.

*This is the "trajectory + compliance parameters" architecture, realized.*

---

## Conditioning: Vision + Force + State

::: {.two-column-balanced}
::: {.compact}
- **RGB image** (84×84) → ResNet-18 encoder + spatial-softmax keypoints.
- **Wrench** (6-D force/torque from the Bota sensor).
- **Proprioceptive state** (12-D: TCP pose + hand joints).
- Short **observation history** (last 2 frames) → predict next action chunk.
:::

![](assets/figures/dp_conditioning.svg){width=100%}
:::

- Force feedback lets the policy *react to contact*, not just to pixels.
- Modular variants exist: **vision-only**, **vision+force**, **vision+force+state**.

---

## Slow Policy, Fast Compliance

![](assets/figures/dp_slow_fast_timeline.svg){width=90%}

- The policy sets **intent and compliance**; the controller handles **contact dynamics**.
- Fast inner loop = stability and safety even if the policy is occasionally noisy.
- Decouples a heavy generative model from a hard real-time control budget.

---

## Fitting Diffusion Into the Control Budget {.smaller}

Problem → levers → result:

- **Budget:** one policy query must fit a ~50 ms slot (20 Hz).
- **Trained** with full DDPM (~100 denoising steps) for quality.
- **Deployed** with **DDIM and far fewer steps (~10)** — fewer network evals.
- **Action chunking:** sample once, execute several steps, re-plan.
- **Systems fix:** a single-threaded executor removed GIL contention → predict time dropped from **~140 ms to ~11 ms**.
- **Result:** steady **~20 Hz** inference with large idle margin.

::: {.equation-card}
$$
T_{\text{query}} = T_{\text{sense}} + T_{\text{enc}} + K\cdot T_{\text{net}} + T_{\text{comm}}
$$
:::

---

## DDPM vs DDIM on the Real Robot {.smaller}

*Same policy, same fridge task, same hardware — only the **deployment sampler** changes.*

```{=html}
<div style="display:flex; gap:56px; justify-content:center; align-items:flex-start; margin-top:6px;">
  <div style="text-align:center;">
    <div style="font-weight:700; color:#dc2626; margin-bottom:6px;">DDPM &middot; ~100 steps</div>
    <video data-autoplay loop muted playsinline controls style="height:560px; border-radius:10px; box-shadow:0 4px 14px rgba(0,0,0,.18);">
      <source src="assets/videos/ddpm_fridge.mp4" type="video/mp4">
    </video>
    <div style="color:#637083; margin-top:8px;">too slow for the loop &rarr; <strong>hesitant, stop-and-go</strong> &middot; ~40&nbsp;s</div>
  </div>
  <div style="text-align:center;">
    <div style="font-weight:700; color:#16a34a; margin-bottom:6px;">DDIM &middot; ~10 steps</div>
    <video data-autoplay loop muted playsinline controls style="height:560px; border-radius:10px; box-shadow:0 4px 14px rgba(0,0,0,.18);">
      <source src="assets/videos/ddim_fridge.mp4" type="video/mp4">
    </video>
    <div style="color:#637083; margin-top:8px;">holds ~20&nbsp;Hz &rarr; <strong>smooth, continuous</strong> &middot; ~16&nbsp;s</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

---

## Collecting Compliant Demonstrations

- Human teleoperates the arm through the **same admittance controller** used at deploy time.
- Each step logs: pose delta, **commanded K and ζ**, hand state, wrench, proprioception, RGB.
- Recorded at **~50 Hz**; **force sensor tared per episode**.
- **"Pre-image" targets:** we record the human's *intended* command + stiffness, **before** the controller transforms it — so the policy learns intent, not the post-controller velocity.
- Example scale: pipe-insertion set ≈ **200 episodes / ~100k frames**.

---

## Where Should Compliance Come From? {.smaller}

*Same task (open a fridge), same data — vary only how compliance is produced.*

| Variant | Policy outputs | Sees force? | Val. loss |
|---|---|:---:|:---:|
| Motion only + force obs | Δpose + hand | ✔ | **0.028** |
| Learned K+ζ, force-blind | Δpose + **K,ζ** + hand | ✗ | 0.030 |
| Motion only, external compliance | Δpose + hand | ✗ | 0.031 |
| Learned K+ζ, force-aware | Δpose + **K,ζ** + hand | ✔ | 0.031 |
| Raw velocity, **no admittance** | velocity + hand | ✗ | 0.042 |
| BC-RNN baseline (not comparable) | Δpose + K,ζ | ✔ | 0.29 |

::: {.warning}
**Validation loss ≠ task success** — these are offline fit metrics; on-robot evaluation is the real test.
:::

<!-- TODO citation: ablation numbers from project experiments. -->

---

## Guardrails on Learned Gains

- Predicted gains are **clipped to an admissible envelope**: e.g. `K ∈ [50, 5000] N/m`, `ζ ∈ [0.3, 5.0]`.
- **Force-safety saturation:** as contact force approaches the limit, `K` is ramped toward 0 → the arm yields instead of pushing harder.
- **Impact / release latch:** on a sudden force drop (a latch or clip letting go), briefly force low `K` + high damping to absorb the transient.
- Hard caps on commanded velocity and displacement at the controller.

::: {.equation-card}
$$
K_{min} \preceq K_d \preceq K_{max},
\qquad
D_{min} \preceq D_d \preceq D_{max}
$$
:::

---

## Case Study — Takeaways

- A diffusion policy can output **motion + compliance (K, ζ)** in one shot, conditioned on vision + force.
- A **slow policy / fast admittance** split makes a heavy generative model deployable on real contact-rich hardware.
- Real-time feasibility is **sampler choice + engineering** (DDIM, chunking, executor fix → ~20 Hz).
- Compliance can be **learned from demonstrations** instead of hand-tuned; safety comes from **bounded gains + a fail-soft controller**.

::: {.takeaway}
Open: offline fit ≠ task success — **on-robot evaluation**, and whether explicit learned gains beat force-aware motion-only, remain to be settled.
:::
